 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","title":"[T1-MSG.6] fcp.mattermost — Mattermost Self-Hosted Collaboration Connector","description":"# Mattermost Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Mattermost is one of the most valuable self-hosted enterprise chat additions because it complements Slack rather than duplicating it: same broad collaboration shape, but with on-prem and regulated-environment deployment models.

## API surface
- REST API for channels, posts, users, teams, files, reactions, and preferences
- WebSocket/event stream for inbound message events, thread updates, reactions, and presence
- Bot token or personal access token authentication depending on deployment model

## Expected operations
- `mattermost.posts.create|get|search|reply`
- `mattermost.channels.list|get|create_direct`
- `mattermost.teams.list|get`
- `mattermost.files.upload|get`
- `mattermost.reactions.add|remove`
- `mattermost.users.list|get`

## V3 considerations
- Bidirectional real-time connector with outbound REST mutations and inbound event handling
- Must model self-hosted base URL, websocket reconnect policy, channel/thread identity, and token redaction carefully
- Manifest must expose configurable host constraints rather than hard-coded SaaS hosts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.1","title":"Mattermost: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix","description":"Define the V3 contract for Mattermost before implementation expands. Pin down the self-hosted deployment assumptions, base URL and auth model, operation inventory across posts or channels or teams or files or reactions or users, and how administrative or membership-changing actions should be risk-classified under V3.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:57:34.401036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:29:04.277550Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:29:04.277145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:57:34.401036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:57:34.401036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:57:34.401036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1836,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for Mattermost:

**Archetype**: Bidirectional + Webhook (self-hosted Slack alternative)
**Auth model**: Personal access token or OAuth 2.0 app. Server URL configurable (no default — self-hosted). Bot account or user account.
**Network**: Mattermost server URL (configurable). TLS recommended but not required (self-hosted). deny_localhost=false (server may be local), deny_private=false.

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| mattermost.send_message | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | mattermost.send |
| mattermost.update_message | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | mattermost.send |
| mattermost.delete_message | Dangerous | High | Strict | mattermost.send |
| mattermost.send_reaction | Risky | Low | BestEffort | mattermost.send |
| mattermost.get_channels | Safe | Low | None | mattermost.read |
| mattermost.get_channel_posts | Safe | Low | None | mattermost.read |
| mattermost.get_user | Safe | Low | None | mattermost.read |
| mattermost.search_posts | Safe | Low | None | mattermost.read |
| mattermost.upload_file | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | mattermost.send |
| mattermost.set_status | Risky | Low | BestEffort | mattermost.admin |
| mattermost.health | Safe | Low | Strict | mattermost.read |

**Webhook inbound**: Mattermost outgoing webhooks or slash commands. Signature verification via webhook secret. Event types: post_created, post_edited, post_deleted.

**Safety policy**: Message deletion is Dangerous (permanent on server). Server version >= 7.0 required for full API surface. WebSocket for real-time events. Rate limits: server-configurable, default 10 req/s.

**Provisioning**: Create bot account or personal access token in Mattermost System Console. Doctor checks: server reachable, token valid, bot has required permissions.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No server administration, no team/channel creation, no user management, no plugin management, no compliance export.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:28:56Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","title":"[T1-MSG.2] fcp.teams — Microsoft Teams Bot + Graph API Connector","description":"Microsoft Teams connector via Bot Framework + Graph API. 320M+ monthly users, dominant enterprise messaging platform. Operations: teams.send_message (channel/chat), teams.send_card (adaptive cards), teams.reply_message, teams.list_channels, teams.list_members, teams.create_channel, teams.get_message, teams.search_messages. Auth: Azure AD OAuth2 with bot registration. Webhook: Bot Framework activity handler for incoming messages/events. Network: graph.microsoft.com + login.microsoftonline.com. Archetype: Bidirectional. Requires Azure AD app registration with Teams bot permissions. 4 subtasks: types/client/error, connector/manifest, webhook/activity handler, tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:35.132813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1783,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:24:49Z"},{"id":1818,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"TEAMS-SPECIFIC TESTING:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1","title":"Teams: tenant topology, app registration, and platform scope","description":"Front-load the Teams-specific platform scoping that sits between the generic V3 contract and the concrete implementation tasks: tenant topology, bot registration model, channel/install prerequisites, conversation identity boundaries, and which Graph surfaces are truly in the first slice. This task exists because Teams is more platform-shaped than a simple chat API and future contributors should not have to reconstruct those deployment assumptions from scratch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:41:11.206840Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:05:14.680575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:05:14.680148Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:41:11.206840Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1852,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Teams platform scoping:

**Tenant topology**: Teams operates within Microsoft 365 tenants. Each tenant is an isolated Azure AD directory. A Teams app (bot) registers in a single tenant or as a multi-tenant app. For FCP, single-tenant registration is the default scope (admin-controlled, no marketplace listing).

**App registration model**: Teams bots use Azure Bot Service, registered in Azure AD. The bot has: Application (client) ID, Client Secret (or certificate), Tenant ID, and a Bot Framework messaging endpoint. Registration via Azure Portal > App Registrations > New Registration > Add Bot Channel.

**Channel topology**:
- Team -> Channels (standard, private, shared)
- Group chats (up to 250 members)  
- 1:1 chats
- Meeting chats (ephemeral, tied to meeting lifecycle)
Bot receives activities via the Bot Framework SDK's messaging endpoint.

**Conversation identity boundaries**:
- conversationId uniquely identifies a chat/channel context
- turnContext.activity contains sender, channel, team, tenant info
- Bot can only respond in conversations where it's installed
- Channel vs. personal scope determined by installation context

**Graph API surfaces (first slice)**:
- Chat messages: GET /teams/{id}/channels/{id}/messages (delegated or application)
- Channel messages: POST to send, PATCH to update
- Teams/channels: list, get (read-only in first slice)
- Users/members: list members of teams/channels
- Proactive messaging: requires serviceUrl + conversationId from prior activity

**Prerequisites**:
- Azure AD tenant with Teams license
- Azure Bot Service registration
- App manifest (teams-app-manifest.json) with bot section

**Archetype**: Database (follows PostgreSQL exemplar pattern)
**Auth model**: Connection string (host/port/user/password) or credential_id (secretless). TLS optional (private network default).
**Network**: Private network / localhost. deny_localhost=false, deny_private=false.

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| mysql.query | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |
| mysql.execute | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | mysql.write |
| mysql.explain | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |
| mysql.schema.tables | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |
| mysql.schema.columns | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |
| mysql.schema.indexes | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |
| mysql.transaction.begin | Safe | Low | None | mysql.write |
| mysql.transaction.commit | Safe | Low | BestEffort | mysql.write |
| mysql.transaction.rollback | Safe | Low | BestEffort | mysql.write |
| mysql.batch | Risky | Medium | None | mysql.write |
| mysql.health | Safe | Low | Strict | mysql.read |

**Safety policy**: Parameterized queries only — raw SQL string interpolation is forbidden. DDL operations (CREATE/ALTER/DROP) deferred to future slice as Dangerous. DELETE queries are Risky in first slice (connector does not parse SQL verb).

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No stored procedure execution, no replication management, no user/permission administration, no MariaDB-specific features.

**Implementation notes**: Uses reqwest via HTTP proxy to MySQL (similar to PostgreSQL PostgREST pattern) or direct mysql_async driver. ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop + ConnectorErrorMapping per standard pattern.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:09:26Z"}]}

Pin down file-path and sandbox boundaries, supported query or schema operations, transaction and mutation safety boundaries, OperationInfo risk semantics, and explicit non-goals for the first safe slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:07:35.308590Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:12:50.398855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:12:50.398432Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.3.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:07:35.308590Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.3.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:07:35.308590Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.3.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:07:35.308590Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1831,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.3.2.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for SQLite:

**Archetype**: Database (unique: no-network pattern)
**Auth model**: File path only. No network credentials. File permissions enforced by OS.
**Network**: NONE. allowed_hosts=[], allowed_ports=[]. This is the only zero-network connector in the database family.

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| sqlite.query | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |
| sqlite.execute | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | sqlite.write |
| sqlite.explain | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |
| sqlite.schema.tables | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |
| sqlite.schema.columns | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |
| sqlite.transaction.begin | Safe | Low | None | sqlite.write |
| sqlite.transaction.commit | Safe | Low | BestEffort | sqlite.write |
| sqlite.transaction.rollback | Safe | Low | BestEffort | sqlite.write |
| sqlite.batch | Risky | Medium | None | sqlite.write |
| sqlite.health | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |
| sqlite.vacuum | Risky | Medium | Strict | sqlite.admin |
| sqlite.pragma | Safe | Low | Strict | sqlite.read |

**Safety policy**: Parameterized queries only. WAL mode recommended. PRAGMA writes deferred to admin capability. ATTACH DATABASE deferred (security surface). File path must be within declared sandbox fs_writable paths.

**Sandbox specifics**: fs_writable includes the database file + WAL/SHM files. deny_exec=true, deny_ptrace=true. No network access at all.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No ATTACH DATABASE (cross-file queries), no extension loading, no custom functions, no FTS5 administration.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","title":"[CROSS-V3] Connector Test Infrastructure: unit/integration/E2E harnesses, detailed logging, compliance suite","description":"# Cross-Cutting Test Infrastructure

## Goal
Establish shared V3-native test patterns and a reusable compliance harness so both new connectors and existing connectors are verified against the current Flywheel Connector Protocol V3 contract instead of legacy ad hoc expectations.

## Why this bead exists early
The workspace spans messaging, cloud, database, developer platform, productivity, commerce, communication, and local-host/process archetypes. Without shared scaffolding, each connector team or retrofit pass would re-invent retry tests, webhook fixtures, manifest validation, readiness checks, and conformance evidence.

## Deliverables

### 1. Connector scaffold template update
Update `fwc` scaffold generation so new connector crates start V3-native by default:
- `ConnectorRuntime`, `RetryLoop`, and `ConnectorErrorMapping` already wired into the generated client and error paths
- wiremock-based unit test scaffolding for success, retry, timeout, cancellation, auth failure, and config validation
- typed `OperationInfo` stubs with `SafetyTier`, `RiskLevel`, and `IdempotencyClass`
- manifest skeleton with capabilities, network constraints, provisioning notes, and readiness hooks

### 2. Shared test helpers in `fcp-testkit`
Provide reusable helpers covering the archetypes in this campaign and in the existing connector inventory:
- HTTP request-response fixtures
- webhook/signature verification fixtures
- bridge/daemon fixtures for Signal-, BlueBubbles-, and similar local-bridge connectors
- database and storage fixture helpers
- assertions for health/readiness/doctor outputs and retry telemetry

### 3. Reusable connector compliance script
Create a reusable compliance entrypoint that:
- boots a connector binary
- exercises handshake/configure/introspect/invoke/shutdown
- validates manifest/readiness surfaces
- emits structured logs/evidence for `fcp-conformance` and E2E review

This must be usable for retrofitting existing connectors, not just for greenfield scaffolds.

### 4. Documentation and operator guidance
Write the campaign-level test playbook with:
- minimum unit/integration/E2E coverage expectations by archetype
- how to run verification through `rch`
- when a bridge dependency requires dedicated integration environments
- how readiness/doctor/conformance evidence should be attached to implementation or remediation beads

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j6ps","title":"[IDEA-22] Device Posture Attestation Enforcement","description":"## Background
The README and RFCs describe optional hardware-backed device posture attestations for high-trust zones, but enforcement is not yet mechanical.

## Goals
- Introduce `DevicePostureAttestation` objects and verification rules.
- Allow zone policies to require hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave).
- Fail closed when posture is missing or invalid.

## Non-goals
- Implementing hardware attestation collection itself for every OS in this iteration (focus on verification and policy).

## Approach
- Define canonical schema for posture attestation.
- Extend enrollment validation and zone policy evaluation to require posture.
- Add test fixtures for valid/invalid posture.

## Risks
- Platform variance in attestation formats; keep schema extensible and versioned.

## Success Criteria
- Zones can enforce posture requirements with stable reason codes.
- Tests cover accept/deny paths.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Zones can enforce posture requirement with stable deny reason.
- Enrollment without posture fails closed in required zones.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j7w1","title":"[IDEA-11] Streaming Health Signal Unification","description":"# Streaming Health Signal Unification

## Goal
Standardize how streaming connectors report health (heartbeat ack, zombie detection, reconnection) so hosts can interpret status consistently.

## Background / Why
Different connectors currently surface health in ad-hoc ways. A unified model improves monitoring, alerting, and auto-restart behavior.

## Scope
- Define health state transitions for streaming connections.
- Provide helper methods for health updates and log emission.

## Deliverables
- `StreamingHealthState` mapping to `HealthSnapshot`.
- Standard log fields for heartbeat/ack counts.

## Tests
- Unit tests for state transitions.
- Integration tests with a simulated streaming loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health signals are deterministic and consistent across connectors.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]

- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.

- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
